justification: All pass criteria met: 4 bounded contexts identified (Route Planning, Fleet Management, Delivery Management, Customer Notification). Cross-context dependencies explicitly called out (e.g., vehicle class enforcement spans Fleet and Delivery contexts, route recalculation triggers depend on Delivery lifecycle events, notification triggers depend on Route state). Vehicle class constraint correctly marked invariant:true (BR-2), stop time correctly marked invariant:false (BR-7), notification window correctly marked invariant:false (BR-6). 9 acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). All 3 route recalculation triggers enumerated (new delivery, failed delivery, manual deviation) in BR-3/4/5.

observations.text: Exceptional bounded context identification — 4 contexts with clear ownership boundaries and explicit integration seam commentary
10 business rules identified, far exceeding the expected 4, with well-reasoned invariant classifications and justifications
9 detailed Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy paths, vehicle class filtering, all recalculation triggers, notifications, and stop time overrides
10 edge cases surfaced including concurrent recalculation, notification deduplication, GPS drift, and shift boundaries — strong defensive analysis
Escalations are well-structured with options and recommendations, correctly identifying multi-driver partitioning and deviation detection as critical ambiguities
Discovered additional invariants not in the rubric (single active route per driver BR-9, single route per delivery BR-10) that are domain-correct

Route recalculation triggers (BR-3/4/5) marked as invariant:false — the rubric expected invariant:true since the system must always recalculate on these events. Bird's justification (they're optimization policies, not data integrity constraints) is defensible but diverges from the expected answer
Cross-context dependencies surfaced as formal escalations for MJ, particularly the multi-driver partitioning problem which is a genuine architectural integration point
